warnings: 1 genuine imaginary mode(s) (|ν| > 50i cm⁻¹) detected — this geometry is a saddle point, NOT a minimum. Thermochemistry is NOT valid here (and could not be computed: Too few vibration modes (5) after selection. {num_vibs} expected). Re-optimize to a true minimum before using these values.

symmetry_number defaulted to 1; if the molecule has rotational symmetry (e.g. H2O σ=2, NH3 σ=3, CH4 σ=12) the entropy and Gibbs energy are over-estimated by R·ln(σ). Re-run with --symmetry-number set to the correct value for your point group.

prose:
Method provenance and computational details:
Property: electronic total energy (Hartree → converted by engine to eV; reporting the engine’s eV value).
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding; no Gaussian basis set).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied ammonia planar transition-state structure at /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/vibrational-analysis-validation/ammonia-planar-ts/ammonia-planar-ts.xyz; no pre-optimization performed (–-no-preopt). The Hessian and frequencies were evaluated directly at this fixed geometry. Result is a first-order saddle (1 genuine imaginary mode).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0 (neutral), 1 (singlet closed-shell).
Software backend: xtb 6.x via chemkit vibrational-analysis skill (`cli … freq --method xtb --no-preopt`).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged; calculation produced 1 imaginary frequency as expected for a TS. Thermochemistry (enthalpy/Gibbs) could not be computed and is undefined for a saddle; only the electronic energy is valid and reported. Density fitting and dispersion are inherent in GFN2-xTB. All engine warnings are echoed above.
Live log: the run streamed to a live .out file in the working directory while executing (path displayed when the job was launched).

Headline result (electronic energy, eV):
-120.14751
